mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate for future corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning, producing negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances or trigger frequency drops below 5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand growth that supports corporate revenue forecasts, driving positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations of RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints that precede corporate margin compression, resulting in negative monthly equity returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of late-cycle observations or regime classification fails to separate return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth reduces near-term corporate earnings expectations, causing immediate downward pressure on equity prices over the following week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 changes.

mechanism_narrative: A widening spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation indicates restrictive real policy that suppresses investment spending, leading to negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive real rate environments yield positive 60-day returns in >55% of cases or the interaction effect vanishes in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth during a post-shock recovery phase signals strong economic momentum that boosts equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns in >60% of recovery period observations or regime filter reduces out-of-sample Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its 12-month moving average signals a supply-side contraction that triggers immediate market sell-offs over the following week.
failure_condition: Triggered instances produce positive 5-day returns in >58% of cases or the moving average crossover generates excessive false signals exceeding 40% of months.
